mission.title: woodpecker-ci: Hashicorp vault as secret storage integration
mission.description: Hashicorp vault as secret storage integration. Requested by 10+ users.

steps.title: Check current woodpecker-ci deployment
steps.description:
Verify your woodpecker-ci version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n woodpecker-ci -l app.kubernetes.io/name=woodpecker-ci
helm list -n woodpecker-ci 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working woodpecker-ci installation.

steps.title: Review woodpecker-ci configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant woodpecker-ci configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n woodpecker-ci -l app.kubernetes.io/name=woodpecker-ci
kubectl get configmap -n woodpecker-ci -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=woodpecker-ci
```
### Clear and concise description of the problem

Hi there, that would be awesome if secrets would be pullable from Hashicorp Vault, by integration like [this](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/ci/examples/authenticating-with-hashicorp-vault/)

###

steps.title: Apply the fix for Hashicorp vault as secret storage integration
steps.description:
Allow users to set / use service extensions (for this PR just config, secrets / registries could be added later on) on a per repo basis. Those extensions are little external webservice which can implement a set of endpoints to replace woodpeckers internal functionality like:
- pipeline config processing

# TODO

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n woodpecker-ci -l app.kubernetes.io/name=woodpecker-ci
kubectl get events -n woodpecker-ci --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Hashicorp vault as secret storage integration" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Allow users to set / use service extensions (for this PR just config, secrets / registries could be added later on) on a per repo basis. Those extensions are little external webservice which can implement a set of endpoints to replace woodpeckers internal functionality like:
- pipeline config processing

# TODO

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with woodpecker-ci installed or the issue environment reproducible.
